title: HTTP server not wired to the router — no route reachable at runtime
evidence: src/book_api_http.erl:12-19 starts inets httpd with modules=[book_api_http], but book_api_http exports only start/0 and stop/0 (no do/1 httpd callback). grep shows book_api_handler is referenced by nothing except its own -module line, so book_api_handler:handle_request/1 (src/book_api_handler.erl:5) is dead code. Every endpoint (R1-R6, R8, R10, /health) is unreachable over HTTP.
suggestion: Export a do/1 callback from book_api_http that adapts inets mod_data to book_api_handler:handle_request/1, or switch to cowboy with a dispatch table.

title: ETS keypos bug: GET/PUT/DELETE by id always return not_found
evidence: src/book_api_db.erl:17 creates the table with {keypos,2} but rows are inserted as {NextId, NewBook} (src/book_api_db.erl:69), so element 2 (the map) becomes the key. ets:lookup(books, Id) with an integer Id (src/book_api_db.erl:42, :81, :103) can never match. get_book_by_id/update_book/delete_book therefore always fall to {error, not_found}.
suggestion: Use the default keypos 1 (drop the {keypos,2} option) so the integer NextId at tuple position 1 is the key.

title: POST /books create path: binary-vs-atom key mismatch always yields 400
evidence: book_api_handler:parse_json builds a map with BINARY keys (src/book_api_handler.erl:210-244, keys via list_to_binary) but book_api_db:create_book/1 pattern-matches ATOM keys #{title:=_,author:=_} (src/book_api_db.erl:50). A parsed body never matches, so it falls to create_book(_) -> {error, invalid_data} -> 400 for all input, valid or not. The correct converter book:parse_json/1 (src/book.erl:64) maps binary->atom keys but the handler never calls it (only the test suite does).
suggestion: Route the handler create path through book:parse_json/1, or key create_book on binary keys #{<<"title">>:=_, ...}.

title: Storage is in-memory ETS + ad-hoc file dump, not SQLite/embedded DB
evidence: src/book_api_db.erl:17 ets:new(books,[named_table,public,{keypos,2}]) — an in-memory ETS table. Persistence is an io_lib:format term dump written to priv/books.dat or /tmp (src/book_api_db.erl:116-147). No DETS, Mnesia, or SQLite. Requirement R7 explicitly excludes 'just in-memory state'.
suggestion: Use DETS or Mnesia (disc_copies) as the embedded store, or bind a SQLite NIF/port driver.

title: Input validation present but not applied on the working path
evidence: book:validate/1 (src/book.erl:4-53) properly rejects missing/empty title/author, but book_api_handler:handle_books_create (src/book_api_handler.erl:53-64) never calls it — it relies on create_book/1's atom-key match, which (per R1-keymismatch) rejects ALL input as invalid_data. Missing-field creates coincidentally return 400, but so do valid ones, and the path is unreachable anyway.
suggestion: Call book:validate/1 in the create handler and distinguish validation errors (400) from success (201).

title: GET /books list logic exists but is unreachable over HTTP
evidence: book_api_db:get_all_books/0 (src/book_api_db.erl:29-35, uses ets:tab2list) and book_api_handler:handle_books_list/1 (src/book_api_handler.erl:36-50) are correct in isolation, but the router is never wired to httpd (see R8-R10-wiring), so the list route cannot be invoked at runtime.
suggestion: Fix the httpd->handler wiring; the list logic itself is sound.

title: ?author= filter implemented but unreachable over HTTP
evidence: books_filter_by_author/2 (src/book_api_handler.erl:144-145) filters the collection, but the handler is never invoked by the httpd (see R8-R10-wiring).

title: JSON responses + status codes built correctly but never emitted
evidence: send_response/2 and the hand-rolled json_encode/1 (src/book_api_handler.erl:120-191) produce JSON bodies with 200/201/204/400/404 codes, but because the handler is unwired (R8-R10-wiring) no response is ever returned by the running server.
suggestion: Wire the handler to httpd (or cowboy); the encoder is functional.

title: GET /health route defined but unreachable
evidence: The {get, "/health"} clause (src/book_api_handler.erl:19-20) returns 200 #{status=>ok}, but the handler is never invoked at runtime (R8-R10-wiring).

title: CRUD 'integration' tests are ?assert(true) stubs — test_coverage=1.0 is misleading
evidence: test/book_api_test.erl:117-125 handler_health_endpoint/0 and handler_books_crud/0 both just call ?assert(true) with a comment 'simplified test'. No test performs an insert->lookup round-trip through ETS, nor drives the HTTP handler. So test_coverage=1.0 does not exercise any of the four confirmed defects.
suggestion: Add a real round-trip test: create_book then get_book_by_id/update_book/delete_book, asserting the created id is retrievable.
